title: Part 3: Designing an agentic eval that means something

cues.label: Seed and check
cues.headline: Score the world, not the words
cues.narration: Seed a fake environment with a known start state, trigger the agent, then check the final state against the expected end state.

cues.label: The state check
cues.headline: Pass and fail become unambiguous
cues.narration: The state check compares the actual end state to the expected end state, so a pass has a single correct answer, just like Gbench.

cues.label: Contamination trap
cues.headline: Leaked answers inflate the score
cues.narration: If the eval leaks into training data the model memorizes it, and that inflated score is worthless.

cues.label: Hold it out
cues.headline: Measure generalization
cues.narration: Hold out a test set the model never trained on, so the score measures generalization rather than memorization.

cues.label: Privacy eval
cues.headline: Respect the disclosure boundary
cues.narration: The context disclosure eval asks the agent to answer each audience and scores whether it stayed within the disclosure boundary.
